title: SpinSmart DNA Extraction From Agarose Gels Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1rfkt","text":"The SpinSmart PCR purification and gel extraction technologies utilize a lysis buffer containing chaotropic salts that allow DNA to bind to a silica membrane. Binding buffer PCR 1 is added to a PCR reaction or agarose gel slice; the mixture is subsequently loaded directly onto SpinSmart PCR Columns.  Salts, enzymes, and other soluble components are washed away with ethanolic PCR 2 Wash buffer. Purified DNA is eluted using PCR 3 Elution buffer (5 mM Tris/HCl, pH 8.5). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7k9bh","text":"The SpinSmart PCR purification and gel extraction technologies utilize a lysis buffer containing chaotropic salts that allow DNA to bind to a silica membrane. Binding buffer PCR 1 is added to a PCR reaction or agarose gel slice; the mixture is subsequently loaded directly onto SpinSmart PCR Columns.  Salts, enzymes, and other soluble components are washed away with ethanolic PCR 2 Wash buffer. Purified DNA is eluted using PCR 3 Elution buffer (5 mM Tris/HCl, pH 8.5). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":278,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8kekp","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"89etq","text":"SpinSmart PCR Kit Specifications ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7ntpc","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"erhqd","text":"SpinSmart PCR kits are designed for DNA purification from TAE/TBE agarose gels and for the direct purification of PCR* products. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fdufl","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2qqjj","text":"SpinSmart PCR buffers are formulated to completely remove primers from PCR* reactions. Small double-stranded DNA fragments still remain bound and are purified with high efficiency. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1j8sl","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"domsj","text":"SpinSmart PCR kits will effectively purify DNA fragments from detergent-rich PCR* reaction buffers.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8lsg8","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"gr6t","text":"DNA absorption to the membrane is pH-dependent. TAE standard gels or reaction mixtures with pH 6-8 should be used for best results. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b98aj","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"72lbl","text":"Both standard and low melting agarose gels can be used. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"tu19","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"21mul","text":"SpinSmart PCR purified DNA fragments are ready to use in downstream applications like automated fluorescent DNA sequencing, PCR (PCR is patented by Roche Diagnostics), ligation reactions, or other types of enzymatic manipulation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4fgdp","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"91boo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"67ukk","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"50u8h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a743d","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"46eob","text":"Removal of small DNA fragments and primer-dimers ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":49}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bbi3j","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aje75","text":"Spin Smart PCR is specially formulated to remove unused, single stranded primers while effectively purifying PCR products down to 60 bp. In some cases, a PCR reaction may yield unwanted small double stranded fragments, such as primer-dimers or small PCR products resulting from unspecific annealing. SpinSmart PCR offers a simple method to remove these products that can interfere with your downstream sequencing or cloning applications. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e87f","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4u98s","text":"By simply diluting PCR 1 Binding Buffer with sterile water, you can decrease the ability of small DNA fragments to bind to the membrane without compromising larger fragment recovery.  A simple dilution series should be tested, ranging from 1:1 – 1:9 (PCR 1:H2O) in order to determine the appropriate cutoff range for your reaction.  As you approach the 1:9 dilution, the larger fragment recovery will sequentially decrease as well. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fb5ss","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c8bts","text":"Rule of Thumb: The smaller the fragment you wish to exclude, the less you will need to dilute the PCR 1 Binding Buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2ke9e","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"60g9n","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f4h5j","text":"Elution procedures","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"15cq2","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b3dq3","text":"DNA should be eluted using the PCR 3 Elution Buffer.  If necessary, sterile water or other low salt elution buffers may be used, however the pH must be in the range of 7.0 – 8.5 for optimal recovery.   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1nu49","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"50msp","text":"Typical recovery of 70-95% can be obtained with DNA fragments between 50bp -10kbp with an elution volume of 15 µl. For larger amounts of DNA (5-15 µg of DNA; from PCR* reactions > 100 µl or gel slices > 200 mg), elution with at least 50 µl of PCR 3 Elution Buffer is recommended.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"832o7","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dbnlm","text":"Pre-warmed PCR 3 Elution Buffer can improve the yields of larger fragments (> 5-10 kbp). Add pre-warmed PCR 3 Elution Buffer (70°C) to the membrane, and incubate for 1-2 minutes, then centrifuge as directed in the standard protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f8b0l","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"erpvu","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"2pt4f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2f5g7","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1btde","text":"Storage and preparation of solutions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":36}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4p5sq","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cu0lm","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2eljl","text":"PCR 1 Wash Buffer contains chaotropic salt. materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"tIzSK","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"BY3QD","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":3698,"guid":"4105F811341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"SpinSmart™ PCR Purification \u0026 Gel Extraction Columns Only, with Collection Tubes, 50 per pack","mol_weight":0,"sku":"CM-500-50","url":"https://www.denvillescientific.com/products/spinsmart%E2%84%A2-pcr-purification-gel-extraction-columns-only--with-collection-tubes--50-per-pack","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":129,"name":"Denville Scientific Inc.","link":"https://www.denvillescientific.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Excise the DNA fragment from an agarose gel using a clean scalpel.
Excise gel slice containing the fragment carefully to minimize the gel volume. Determine the weight of the gel slice and transfer to a clean tube.

For each 100 mg of agarose gel add 200 µl PCR 1 Binding Buffer. For gels containing > 2% agarose, double the volume of PCR 1 Binding Buffer. Max Gel slice size is 400 mg for normal gel (<2%) or 200 mg for high percentage gel ( > 2%).
Incubate sample at 50°C until gel slice is fully dissolved ( 5-10 min ). Vortex the sample briefly every 2-3 min during incubation. 00:05:00
Place a SpinSmart PCR Binding Column (yellow ring) into a Collection Tube (2 ml) and load the sample.
Centrifuge for 1 min at 11,000 x g. 00:01:00
Discard flow-through and place the SpinSmart PCR Binding Column back into the Collection Tube.
Centrifuge for 1 min at 11,000 x g. 00:01:00

Discard flow-through and place the SpinSmart PCR Binding Column back into the Collection Tube. Optional: To prevent salt carryover for sensitive procedures, add an additional 200 µl PCR 2 Wash Buffer and repeat wash steps 8-10.

Centrifuge for 2 min at 11,000 x g to remove PCR 2 Wash Buffer 00:02:00 Residual ethanol from Wash Buffer PCR 2 can inhibit subsequent reactions and must be removed in this step. Complete EtOH removal can be achieved by incubation of SpinSmart PCR Columns for 2-5 min at 70°C prior to elution.
Place the SpinSmart PCR Binding Column into a clean 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube (not provided)
Add 15 - 50 µl PCR 3 Elution Buffer and incubate at room temperature for 1 min to increase the yield of eluted DNA. 00:01:00
Centrifuge for 1 min at 11,000 x g . 00:01:00 Prewarmed PCR 3 Elution Buffer (70°C) can be used to increase the yield of larger fragments (> 5-10 kbp).
